entities.name: SemanticAnalysis
observations: [LLM] The SemanticAnalysis component employs a multi-agent architecture, utilizing agents such as the OntologyClassificationAgent, SemanticAnalysisAgent, and CodeGraphAgent, to perform tasks such as code analysis, ontology classification, and insight generation. The OntologyClassificationAgent, for instance, is implemented in the file integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/agents/ontology-classification-agent.ts and is responsible for classifying observations against the ontology system. [LLM] The component leverages the GraphDatabaseAdapter, implemented in the file storage/graph-database-adapter.js, for Graphology+LevelDB persistence with automatic JSON export sync. This adapter provides a standardized interface for interacting with the graph database, allowing agents such as the CodeGraphAgent to construct a knowledge graph of code entities using Memgraph and Tree-sitter AST parsing. The use of a graph database enables efficient querying and traversal of the code entities, facilitating the generation of insights and the detection of relationships between entities.

[LLM] The SemanticAnalysisAgent, implemented in the file integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/agents/semantic-analysis-agent.ts, performs comprehensive semantic analysis of code files and git history. This agent utilizes the Tree-sitter AST parsing library to analyze the code structure and extract relevant information, such as function calls, variable declarations, and control flow. The agent also integrates with the OntologyClassificationAgent to classify the extracted information against the ontology system, enabling the component to generate insights and detect patterns in the codebase.

[LLM] The component implements work-stealing concurrency via a shared atomic index counter in the runWithConcurrency function, allowing multiple agents to execute concurrently and improving the overall performance of the component. This concurrency mechanism is essential for handling large-scale codebases, as it enables the component to process multiple files and entities simultaneously, reducing the overall processing time. The use of a shared atomic index counter ensures that the agents can safely access and update the index, preventing conflicts and ensuring correct execution.

[LLM] The ContentValidationAgent, implemented in the file integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/agents/content-validation-agent.ts, is responsible for validating entity content and detecting staleness in observations and insights. This agent checks the entity content against a set of predefined rules and constraints, ensuring that the content is consistent and up-to-date. The agent also integrates with the SemanticAnalysisAgent to detect staleness in observations and insights, enabling the component to update and refresh the knowledge graph as necessary. entities.name: LLMAbstraction
observations: [LLM] The LLMAbstraction component uses a provider-agnostic approach, allowing for easy switching between different LLM providers. This is achieved through the ProviderRegistry class (lib/llm/provider-registry.js), which manages the different LLM providers and their configurations. For instance, the AnthropicProvider class (lib/llm/providers/anthropic-provider.ts) is used to interact with the Anthropic API, while the DMRProvider class (lib/llm/providers/dmr-provider.ts) is used for local LLM inference. The use of a provider registry enables the component to be highly flexible and scalable, as new providers can be easily added or removed without affecting the overall architecture.

[LLM] The component employs a circuit breaker pattern using the CircuitBreaker class (lib/llm/circuit-breaker.js) to detect and prevent cascading failures when interacting with the LLM providers. This is crucial in ensuring the reliability and availability of the component, especially in scenarios where the LLM providers may be experiencing downtime or high latency. The circuit breaker pattern helps to prevent the component from repeatedly attempting to access a failed provider, thereby reducing the likelihood of cascading failures. The CircuitBreaker class is used in conjunction with the LLMService class (lib/llm/llm-service.ts) to handle mode routing, caching, and circuit breaking.

[LLM] The LLMAbstraction component uses a mock mode, implemented through the MockProvider class (lib/llm/providers/mock-provider.js), which generates plausible data for testing purposes without actual API calls. This is useful for testing the component in isolation, without relying on external dependencies. The MockProvider class is designed to mimic the behavior of the actual LLM providers, allowing developers to test the component's functionality and performance without incurring the costs associated with API calls. The use of a mock mode also enables developers to test the component's error handling and edge cases, ensuring that it behaves as expected in different scenarios.

[LLM] The component supports configuration loading from YAML files, as seen in the loadConfig function (lib/llm/config.js). This allows for flexible and customizable provider configurations, enabling developers to easily switch between different providers or adjust the component's behavior to suit their needs. The loadConfig function is used to load the configuration from a YAML file, which is then used to initialize the provider registry and configure the component's behavior. The use of YAML files for configuration loading provides a simple and intuitive way to manage the component's configuration, making it easier to deploy and manage in different environments.

[LLM] The LLMAbstraction component handles mode routing, caching, and circuit breaking through the LLMService class (lib/llm/llm-service.ts). This class serves as the main entry point for all LLM operations, providing a unified interface for interacting with the different LLM providers. The LLMService class uses the provider registry to determine the appropriate provider to use for a given request, and then routes the request to the selected provider. The class also implements caching and circuit breaking mechanisms to optimize performance and reliability. The use of a single entry point for all LLM operations simplifies the component's architecture and makes it easier to manage and maintain.

[LLM] The component's architecture is designed to be highly modular and scalable, with each provider being implemented as a separate class. For example, the AnthropicProvider class (lib/llm/providers/anthropic-provider.ts) is responsible for interacting with the Anthropic API, while the DMRProvider class (lib/llm/providers/dmr-provider.ts) handles local LLM inference. This modularity enables developers to easily add or remove providers without affecting the overall architecture of the component. The use of separate classes for each provider also makes it easier to test and maintain the component, as each provider can be tested and updated independently.
